topic: Temperature Tower for Elegoo PLA on the Sprite Extruder Pro Direct Drive (Hoverfly)
summary:
A temperature tower is a single vertical print whose horizontal bands are each extruded at a different nozzle temperature, letting you find the lowest temperature that still gives strong layer bonding and clean detail. For Elegoo PLA the manufacturer range is roughly 190-220C (Elegoo states 190-230C usable, ~205C nominal). For Hoverfly I recommend sweeping 220 -> 190C in 5C bands (i.e. 220/215/210/205/200/195/190). Print from HOT at the bottom to COLD at the top: a band that is too cold cannot extrude/adhere and would otherwise ruin everything above it, so putting the hottest band first protects the print.

Direct-drive specifics: the Sprite Extruder Pro has a very short filament path and low ooze pressure compared with a Bowden setup, so PLA generally tolerates and benefits from the LOWER end of the range (often 200-210C) without under-extrusion, and stringing is easier to suppress. Two caveats unique to this machine: (1) LIN_ADVANCE is enabled but ADVANCE_K = 0.0 (untuned). Linear Advance interacts with how the nozzle pressurizes/depressurizes, so do the temperature tower FIRST, then tune Linear Advance K at the chosen temperature; re-checking afterward is fine but temperature is the more fundamental variable. (2) The part-cooling fan and E0 auto fan (FAN1 @ 50C, full speed) strongly affect bridging/overhang results, so keep fan settings identical across the whole tower or you will misattribute droop to temperature.

How to judge each band: (a) Layer adhesion - after the print cools, try to snap/flex each band; cold bands delaminate or split between layers, good bands break across layers, not between them. (b) Stringing/oozing - look for fine hairs between the tower's two halves/pillars; hotter bands string more, cooler bands are cleaner. (c) Bridging - inspect the underside of any horizontal span for sag; excessive sag at high temp means the melt is too fluid / cooling can't keep up. (d) Overhangs - look for curling and rough, droopy edges; these worsen as temperature rises. (e) Surface finish & dimensional detail - hot = glossy but blobby/rounded corners; cold = matte, crisp, but risk of gaps/under-extrusion. Pick the LOWEST temperature that still passes the adhesion snap test and shows no gaps/under-extrusion - that minimizes stringing and maximizes overhang/bridge quality while keeping parts strong.

Slicer setup: the recommended workflow is to set the filament/print temperature to the HOTTEST band (220C), slice, then inject a temperature change at the Z height of each subsequent band. In Cura use Extensions -> Post Processing -> Modify G-Code -> Add a script -> "ChangeAtZ", enable "Change Extruder 1 Temp," set the Z height (or layer) and target temp, set "Apply to: this layer and all subsequent layers," and add one ChangeAtZ entry per band. In PrusaSlicer/SuperSlicer/Orca, use the layer/height slider in the sliced G-code Preview, right-click the "+" at the band height, choose Add Custom G-Code, and enter M104 S<temp> (no wait, keeps printing) - e.g. M104 S215, then M104 S210 at the next band, etc. Use M104 (not M109) for the in-print steps so the print does not pause/wait at each band. Most popular tower STLs (e.g. gaaZolee's "Smart compact temperature calibration tower") have bands sized for 5C steps and many ship with a matching Cura script or pre-baked G-code.
stlModel: gaaZolee "Smart compact temperature calibration tower" (5C bands; the de facto standard temp tower, available on Thingiverse/Printables). Any banded temp tower with bridging + overhang + stringing-bridge features works.
procedure: 1. Download a banded temperature tower STL (gaaZolee Smart compact temperature calibration tower or similar with overhang/bridge/stringing features). 2. In your slicer set the nozzle temperature to the HOTTEST band, 220C. Bed 60C, part-cooling fan at your normal PLA setting (e.g. 100% after the first few layers) and KEEP IT CONSTANT for the whole tower. 3. Slice. 4. Inject temperature steps DOWN by 5C at each band height: Cura -> Extensions > Post Processing > Modify G-Code > ChangeAtZ (enable Change Extruder 1 Temp, set height + temp, Apply to this and all subsequent layers), one entry per band. PrusaSlicer/Orca/SuperSlicer -> in G-code Preview, right-click the + on the height slider at each band, Add Custom G-Code, enter M104 S<temp> (use M104, not M109, so it does not pause). Bands: 220 (base) ->215->210->205->200->195->190 at the top. 5. Print the whole tower in one go. 6. Evaluate, from bottom (hot) to top (cold): GOOD band = strong layer adhesion (snaps across layers, not between them), no gaps or under-extrusion, minimal stringing, clean non-drooping overhangs, crisp corners, smooth bridge undersides. BAD/too HOT = heavy stringing/hairs, blobs, glossy over-rounded corners, sagging bridges/overhangs, curling. BAD/too COLD = layers peel/delaminate, visible gaps between lines, rough/under-extruded surface, weak snap. 7. PASS criterion: choose the LOWEST temperature that still passes the layer-adhesion snap test AND shows no under-extrusion/gaps. For the Sprite direct drive on Elegoo PLA this is typically 200-210C. 8. Set that temperature in your default PLA profile, then proceed to tune Linear Advance (ADVANCE_K) at that temperature.

recommendations.title: Sweep 220C down to 190C in 5C bands, hottest at the bottom
recommendations.recommendedValue: Bands: 220 / 215 / 210 / 205 / 200 / 195 / 190 C (hot at base, cold at top)
recommendations.howToApply: Slice at 220C; inject a -5C step at each band height. Cura: ChangeAtZ post-process script per band. PrusaSlicer/Orca: right-click the height slider + > Add Custom G-Code > M104 S<temp> at each band.
recommendations.rationale: Elegoo's stated PLA range is ~190-230C (nominal ~205C); 190-220 covers the practical window for a direct drive. Printing hottest-first prevents a too-cold band (which under-extrudes/fails to adhere) from wrecking everything above it. 5C steps are the community standard and the resolution most tower STLs are designed for.

recommendations.title: Use M104 (not M109) for in-print band changes
recommendations.recommendedValue: M104 S<temp> at each band height
recommendations.howToApply: PrusaSlicer/Orca/SuperSlicer: G-code Preview > right-click + on height slider > Add Custom G-Code > 'M104 S215' (then S210, S205... at successive bands). Cura: ChangeAtZ handles this automatically when you set the temp per height.
recommendations.rationale: M104 sets the new temperature without halting motion, so the print transitions between bands continuously; M109 waits and would pause/dwell at each band, leaving an artifact and slowing the print. The hotend reaches the lower setpoint quickly enough during the band that no wait is needed when stepping DOWN.

recommendations.title: Cura setup via ChangeAtZ post-processing script
recommendations.recommendedValue: One ChangeAtZ entry per band, 'Apply to: this layer and all subsequent layers'
recommendations.howToApply: Cura: Extensions > Post Processing > Modify G-Code > Add a script > ChangeAtZ. Enable 'Change Extruder 1 Temp', set the band height (mm) or layer number and target temp, and set Apply-to to successive layers. Repeat for each band.
recommendations.rationale: ChangeAtZ is the supported, repeatable way to do per-height temperature changes in Cura without hand-editing G-code, and 'successive layers' makes the new temp persist through the whole band.

recommendations.title: Judge each band by adhesion, stringing, bridging, overhang, and detail
recommendations.recommendedValue: Pick the LOWEST temp that still passes the layer-adhesion snap test with no under-extrusion
recommendations.howToApply: After cooling: flex/snap each band (good breaks across layers, bad delaminates between them); inspect for hairs/stringing (worse when hot); check bridge undersides and overhang edges for sag/curl (worse when hot); check corners/surface (hot=glossy/blobby/rounded, cold=matte/crisp but risk gaps).
recommendations.rationale: Lower temperature reduces stringing/ooze and improves overhang and bridge quality, but too low causes weak interlayer adhesion and under-extrusion. The optimal point is the lowest temperature that still bonds layers fully - this is the standard tower-reading methodology.

recommendations.title: Keep cooling-fan settings constant across the whole tower (direct-drive auto-fan caveat)
recommendations.currentValue: E0_AUTO_FAN @ 50C full speed; part-cooling per slicer profile
recommendations.recommendedValue: Fixed part-cooling fan % for the entire tower; do not let it ramp between bands
recommendations.howToApply: In the slicer, set a constant fan speed for the print and avoid auto fan-speed-by-layer-time features for this test. The E0 auto fan (FAN1_PIN @ 50C, speed 255) is the hotend heatsink fan and is fine; ensure it is the PART-cooling fan that stays constant.
recommendations.rationale: Bridging and overhang quality - two of the things you judge per band - are dominated by part cooling. If fan speed varies between bands you will wrongly attribute droop or curl to temperature. Holding cooling constant isolates temperature as the only variable.

recommendations.title: Run the temperature tower BEFORE tuning Linear Advance (ADVANCE_K)
recommendations.recommendedValue: Lock in temperature first, then calibrate ADVANCE_K at that temperature
recommendations.howToApply: Finish the temp tower, set your chosen PLA temp in the profile, then run a Linear Advance / Pressure Advance tower at that fixed temperature to find K (Marlin M900 K / Ellis tower method).
recommendations.rationale: Temperature changes melt viscosity and therefore the nozzle pressure dynamics that Linear Advance compensates. Tuning K at one temperature then changing temperature invalidates K. Temperature is the more fundamental variable, so fix it first - this matters here because ADVANCE_K is still 0.0 and untuned on a direct drive that benefits from LA.

imageNeeds: Photo of a printed temperature tower with each 5C band labeled (220 at base down to 190 at top) showing stringing/blobs at hot bands and clean detail at cooler bands
Cura screenshot of Extensions > Post Processing > Modify G-Code with a ChangeAtZ script configured (Change Extruder 1 Temp, height, target temp, apply to successive layers)
PrusaSlicer/Orca screenshot of the G-code Preview height slider with a right-click Add Custom G-Code dialog showing M104 S215
Close-up comparison of a layer-adhesion snap test: cold band delaminating between layers vs a good band breaking across layers
Annotated close-up of bridge underside and overhang edge showing sag/curl on a too-hot band vs clean on an optimal band
